mechanism_narrative: Positive 3-month momentum in INDPRO signals expanding real output and improving operating leverage, causing higher expected equity cash flows and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month INDPRO growth does not produce positive 20-day forward return spreads versus lower INDPRO growth in walk-forward tests, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS crosses above 2.25% from below, the higher policy-rate level raises discount rates and tightens financial conditions, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations have positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if the mean triggered return is not below the unconditional mean at p <= 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE of more than 0.2 percentage points over 3 months signals labor-market deterioration and recession-risk repricing, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.2pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward returns following the trigger are not lower than non-triggered periods, or if the trigger has a positive hit rate above 55% in walk-forward evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: Positive RSAFS momentum combined with non-accelerating CPIAUCSL signals real consumption growth without inflation pressure, improving earnings expectations and causing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month growth > 0 and CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth <= its prior 12-month growth
failure_condition: Reject if the joint signal fails to outperform cases with positive RSAFS momentum and accelerating CPIAUCSL, or if its 20-day mean excess return is not positive at p <= 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in HOUST more than 10% below its 12-month average signals weakening interest-sensitive demand and future cyclical slowdown, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses below 90% of its 12-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if triggered HOUST breakdowns are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if average triggered returns exceed unconditional returns.

mechanism_narrative: Rising FEDFUNDS together with elevated CPIAUCSL inflation indicates restrictive policy responding to inflation, producing near-term valuation compression and negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS 3-month change > 0 and CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth > 2%
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction term between FEDFUNDS change and CPIAUCSL inflation is not negative for 5-day returns, or if triggered 5-day returns are not below baseline in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Positive 3-month PAYEMS growth signals expanding labor income and resilient household spending, causing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days through improved revenue expectations.
failure_condition: Reject if higher PAYEMS 3-month growth is not associated with higher 60-day forward returns, or if the top-minus-bottom PAYEMS momentum return spread is negative over two consecutive 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Positive GDPC1 growth while FEDFUNDS remains below 3% signals real demand expansion under nonrestrictive policy, causing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition does not deliver positive 60-day average returns or if cases with GDPC1 growth and FEDFUNDS below 3% fail to outperform cases with FEDFUNDS at or above 3%.
